channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: McIlroy makes sluggish start as DeChambeau chases Suber at The Open
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Bryson DeChambeau and defending champion Scottie Scheffler both made encouraging starts to The 154th Open, as Rory McIlroy’s major hopes suffered an early setback.

item.title: C’River unveils campaign to curb preeclampsia deaths
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Cross River State launches a weeklong campaign to curb preeclampsia deaths, a leading cause of maternal and neonatal fatalities. Early detection and qualit

item.title: Tinubu approves Lagos-Ibadan expressway reconstruction with concrete pavement

item.description:
President Tinubu has approved the reconstruction of the Lagos-Ibadan Expressway with durable concrete pavement, ensuring a long-lasting solution for this v
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-approves-lagos-ibadan-expressway-reconstruction-with-concrete-pavement/

item.title: INEC considers mock presidential poll before 2027 election
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
INEC is considering a mock presidential election and technology audit before the 2027 general election to enhance reliability and address 2023 concerns.
Read More: https://punchng.com/inec-considers-mock-presidential-poll-before-2027-election/

item.title: NHIA deploys compliance officers to tackle illegal charges in hospitals
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The NHIA has deployed compliance officers to accredited hospitals to tackle illegal charges and improve service delivery for enrollees.

item.title: Lagos releases 2026/2027 academic calendar, fixes September 14 resumption

item.description:
The Lagos State Government has released the 2026/2027 academic calendar for primary and secondary schools, fixing September 14, 2026, as the resumption dat
Read More: https://punchng.com/lagos-releases-2026-2027-academic-calendar-fixes-september-14-resumption/

item.title: Delap Won’t Be A Bad Striker For Everton –Aiyegbeni
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Former Super Eagles striker Yakubu Aiyegbeni has expressed mixed feelings, stating Liam Delap is not a bad player to bring in to Everton. In an interview with ToffeeWeb, the former Everton striker acknowledged the massive leap Delap took moving from Ipswich to Chelsea and noted his subsequent strugg

item.title: Tuchel defends tactics and blames England's 'DNA' for World Cup exit
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Thomas Tuchel says he is "100 per cent" committed to continuing his role as England boss into the next European Championships in two years, and he has hit back at critics who have questioned his tactics, saying instead it is a problem with English football's "DNA".

item.title: World Cup: England, France players don’t want to play third-place match – Tuchel
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: England manager, Thomas Tuchel, has stated that both his team and the French players do not want to play in the 2026 World Cup third place match. He said they will rather play in final on Sunday in New Jersey than in the third-place playoff scheduled for Saturday in Miami. The Three Lions were just

item.title: Canada wildfires spread smoke across North America, prompting air quality alerts
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Smoke from more than 800 blazes has prompted air quality concerns in Toronto, New York and large parts of Michigan, Minneapolis and Minnesota.

item.title: Court Bars FRSC Officials From State Roads
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The Federal High Court sitting in Kano has ruled that the Federal Road Safety Commission (FRSC) lacks the authority to operate on state and local government roads. The court has also declared the actions in Kano metropolis unlawful and a violation of citizens’ fundamental rights. Delivering judgment

item.title: China, Russia, Brazil, 26 other countries to establish World AI Cooperation Organization
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A total of 29 countries have signed an agreement in Shanghai, China, to establish the World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organization (WAICO). The post China, Russia, Brazil, 26 other countries to establish World AI Cooperation Organization appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Nigeria Secures Global Passport Verification As NIS Joins ICAO Trust Network
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria has recorded a major breakthrough in international travel security after successfully integrating its passport authentication system into the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO) Public Key Directory (PKD), a development expected to eliminate verification challenges previously en

item.title: Jonathan’s son graduates from UK university
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Former President Goodluck Jonathan’s son, Ariwera Jonathan, graduates from Manchester Metropolitan University. Read about Jonathan’s son graduates and fami
Read More: https://punchng.com/jonathans-son-graduates-from-uk-university/

item.title: Trump suspends teleprompter operator for allegedly betting on speeches
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: US President Donald Trump has suspended a teleprompter operator over allegations he placed bets with a prediction market on the content of Trump's speeches, the White House said Thursday. The post Trump suspends teleprompter operator for allegedly betting on speeches appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: INEC seeks UK support for 2027 election preparations
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: INEC has informed the United Kingdom government of its dependence on deeper collaboration in security, technology, cybersecurity and voter education as it intensifies preparations for the 2027 general elections. The post INEC seeks UK support for 2027 election preparations appeared first on The Sun

item.title: Stakeholders back NCC rules, seek tougher telecom enforcement
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Stakeholders in Nigeria’s telecom sector endorse NCC’s draft business rules for Mobile Virtual Network Operators (MVNOs) and urge stronger enforcement.
Read More: https://punchng.com/stakeholders-back-ncc-rules-seek-tougher-telecom-enforcement/

item.title: Why are CD sales suddenly growing again?
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: CD sales are apparently going up, reportedly thanks to fans realizing they're an affordable way to support their favorite artists. According to a new report from research firm Luminate, 16.3 million CDs were sold in the first half of 2026 in the US, a 16 percent increase year-over-year. The growth i

item.title: ADC remains surest path to Igbo presidency — Kenneth Okonkwo
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A chieftain of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Kenneth Okonkwo, has said the party offers the strongest opportunity for an Igbo presidency, insisting that its current political arrangement is better positioned to achieve the long-sought goal. The post ADC remains surest path to Igbo presidenc

item.title: Some England players disappointed at tactics as Tuchel questions team's DNA
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: BBC Sport understands that Thomas Tuchel's defensive tactics in the World Cup semi-final defeat to Argentina are being hotly debated by the England squad.

item.title: ‘There was plan to assassinate me’ – Peter Obi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, in next year’s general elections, Peter Obi, has alleged that there was a plan to assassinate him in April 2025, when he planned to visit Benue State  Obi made this allegation at the ongoing high-level roundtable discussion wit

item.title: Zambian Former Vice President Guy Scott Dies At 82
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former Zambian Vice President Guy Scott, who briefly became Africa’s only white head of state after the end of apartheid, has died at the age of 82. The Zambian government announced his death on Wednesday, stating that the former acting president passed away at his farm in Lusaka, the country’s capi

item.title: Africa needs institutions that build technology not dependence
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Every technological revolution arrives with remarkably similar promises. Radio was expected to democratise education. Television would transform learning. The internet read more Africa needs institutions that build technology not dependence

item.title: Our Advocacy Paid Off, Says Ugochinyere As Tinubu Approves S’East Road
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The House of Representatives member for Ideato North and South Federal Constituency of Imo State, Ikenga Ugochinyere, said President Bola Tinubu’s approval of the Oba-Nnewi-Uga-Ihube Road project is the outcome of years of advocacy for federal intervention on the deteriorating highway. The Oba

item.title: 75-Year-Old Man Sentenced To 10 Years In Prison For Defiling Minor In Rivers
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A Rivers State Family Court sitting in Port Harcourt has sentenced a 75-year-old man, Promise Chukwumatti, to 10 years imprisonment without an option of fine for sexually abusing an 11-year-old girl. The court, presided over by Justice Rita Oguguo, convicted Chukwumatti after finding that the prosec

According to the charge filed by the Kano State government, the defendant allegedly killed his wife before

item.title: FIFA probes Argentina over Falklands banner after England defeat
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: FIFA is currently "reviewing match reports" before deciding whether to punish Argentina after players celebrated their dramatic 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-final victory over England with a banner supporting the country's claim to the Falkland Islands. item.title: 2027: INEC considers full systems audit, mock presidential election
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, has moved to consider conducting a comprehensive audit of all its electoral technology systems, alongside a mock presidential election. This is as part of measures to boost preparations for the 2027 general election. The Chairman of the Commission

item.title: Alcaraz Sets Sights On Cincinnati Comeback As US Open Looms
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Carlos Alcaraz has officially entered next month’s Cincinnati Open, signaling his return to competition after a wrist injury sidelined him since April. The world number three and seven-time Grand Slam champion was forced to withdraw from both the French Open and Wimbledon, missing his title de

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Peoples Redemption Party (PRP), Bauchi State Chapter, has announced that it has successfully uploaded the names of all its National Assembly candidates for the 2027 general elections to the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) portal. The party said the uploaded list comprises three

item.title: Turkey: Galatasaray manager, Buruk approves Osimhen’s row as new captain
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Galatasaray manager Okan Buruk has expressed his intention to appoint Nigerian striker Victor Osimhen as one of the club’s new captains for the 2026/2027 season. Osimhen, who made a permanent move to the Turkish champions last summer, enjoyed an exceptional season and was instrumental in Galat

item.title: Just In: US forces launch new strikes against Iran

item.description: US forces launched strikes against Iran for a sixth night in a row on Thursday, the US military said. The post Just In: US forces launch new strikes against Iran appeared first on Vanguard News.

Justice M. Zubairu convicted the duo on Thursday after they pleaded gu

David Umahi, and called for an independent forensic autopsy into the death of Mary Habila, whose body was reportedly discovered within the premises of the minister’s offic

Large protests erupted in several Ukrainian cities against the removal of

item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has formally presented Vice President Kashim Shettima with a certificate confirming his nomination as his running mate for the 2027 presidential election. The presentation followed the confirmation of Shettima by the All Progressives Congress (APC) as Tinubu’s vice-presid

item.description: “It is a growing cardiovascular threat”: Chicago, Detroit, Minneapolis and New York City are considered some of the world’s most polluted cities Thursday.

item.description: Mary Amaechi died peacefully on Thursday 16th July, 2026 aged 89, the media office of the ADC vice presidential candidate said in a statement.

item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.title: SpaceX Post-Listing Collapse Threatens IPO Market’s AI Euphoria
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: SpaceX’s fall from its post-listing peak to below its IPO price in just a month poured cold water on the market for newly public companies, dragging a key gauge of this year’s debuts down with it.

item.title: Cardoso: Nigeria’s Net External Reserves Surge Above $40bn
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The country’s net external reserves, which stood at about $3 billion, when the current management of the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) assumed office in October 2023, have now risen above $40 billion, the apex bank Governor, Olayemi Cardoso, has said. Cardoso, who disclosed this at the BusinessDay C

item.title: Court restrains FRSC from enforcing traffic rules on Kano State roads
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A Federal High Court in Kano has ruled that officials of the Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) have no legal authority to carry out enforcement on state and local government roads in Kano. In a judgment delivered on Thursday, Justice M. S. Shuaibu held that the commission acted outside its power

item.description: The Bashar Chiefdom in Wase Local Government Area has lauded Governor Caleb Manasseh Mutfwang for reinstating His Royal Highness, Alhaji Abdullahi Idris, as the Rekna of Bashar, the highest traditional ruler in the district. The traditional ruler was suspended on October 2, 2024, but the governor re

item.title: BREAKING: Dangote: Nigerian filling stations increase fuel price
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigerian filling stations increased their Premium Motor Spirit, PMS, pump prices on Thursday. A market survey by DAILY POST showed that AA Rano, Ranoil and other filling stations raised their fuel price to N1,220 per litre, up from between N1,170 and N1,180 per litre in Abuja and its environs. This

item.title: No Plumptre, Experienced Core, New Faces… Assessing Super Falcons’ Squad For WAFCON 2026
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: With an 11th continental title on the line and a ticket to the 2027 World Cup in Brazil at stake, the mission for the Super Eagles at WAFCON is clear. The post No Plumptre, Experienced Core, New Faces… Assessing Super Falcons’ Squad For WAFCON 2026 appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Arsenal Agree Fee With Club Brugge To Sign Greek Winger Tzolis
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Arsenal have agreed a deal with Club Brugge for winger Christos Tzolis, Sky Sports News reports. The fee is understood to be £34m, which is a record for the Belgian Pro League. Once Tzolis heard that the Premier League champions were interested, he only wanted Arsenal. Sky Sports News understands Ar

item.title: Ebola spreading more quickly in DRC, while Uganda is close to being virus-free
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Uganda ⁠⁠discharges last-remaining patient, as WHO says Ebola has 'expanded faster than any previous outbreak' in DRC.

item.title: Police, Customs to conduct live shooting exercises in Kano
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Kano State Police Command has announced that the Nigeria Police Force and the Nigeria Customs Service Training College will hold separate live-ammunition shooting exercises at Hawan Kalibawa in Dawakin Tofa Local Government Area. According to a statement issued on Thursday by the Polic

item.title: Police begin nationwide illegal arms mop-up ahead of 2027 elections
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Ahead of the 2027 general elections, the Inspector-General of Police (IGP), Olatunji Disu, said the Nigeria Police Force has commenced a programme to mop up illegal arms across the country. The post Police begin nationwide illegal arms mop-up ahead of 2027 elections appeared first on The Sun Nigeria

item.title: ‘Phantom’ Council DG spotted boarding private jet after arrest
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Adeniyi Adeyemi, controversial Director-General of the Presidential Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), an organisation authorities have described as a fake Federal Government agency, was spotted boarding a private jet to Abuja. ‎ In a video seen by Daily Trust, on Thursday, Adeyemi was seen sur

item.title: Mary Habila: Suspend Umahi now, APM tells Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Allied People's Movement, APM, has called on President Bola Tinubu to immediately suspend the Minister of Works, Engr. Dave Umahi, pending what it described as an open, independent and transparent inquest into the death of Mary Habila, a physiotherapist reportedly found dead at the minister's re

item.title: Twist in El-Rufai’s trial as lawyer disowns recusal applications
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: ICPC said in the interest of fair hearing, it did not oppose the application for an adjournment.

item.title: Gov Bala Mohammed Seeks Solar, Water Solutions From Tunisia
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Governor Bala Mohammed has sought Tunisia’s support in developing off-grid solar power and sustainable water supply systems as part of efforts to improve public services in Bauchi State. The governor made the request during a bilateral meeting with the Tunisian Ambassador to Nigeria, Mohsen An

item.title: Regulatory Conflicts Threatening Electricity Market Reforms, Says Minister
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Minister of Power, Chief Joseph Tegbe, has urged stakeholders in the Nigerian Electricity Supply Industry (NESI) to embrace collaboration over competition. He warned that regulatory conflicts among institutions could undermine the success of Nigeria’s decentralised electricity market. Spea

item.title: Mary Habila wanted to have her bath before she died — Umahi
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Speaking with journalists for the first time since the incident on Thursday, Umahi also revealed that Habila had The post Mary Habila wanted to have her bath before she died — Umahi appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Gbajabiamila sues Prince Matthew for N15bn over alleged 48% kickback claim
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Chief of Staff to the President, Femi Gbajabiamila, has filed a N15 billion defamation suit against Prince Adeniyi Adeyemi Matthew over allegations that he demanded a 48 per cent kickback from a N27.3 billion take-off grant approved for a federal agency. The post Gbajabiamila sues Prince Matthew

item.title: Fresh Crisis Rocks Kaduna ADC As Salihu Lukman Dumps Party
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A fresh crisis has hit the Kaduna State chapter of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) following the decision of one of the party’s founding members, Dr. Salihu Mohammed Lukman, to dump the party over what he described as the hijack of its structures and hostile treatment by former Ka

item.title: I’ve never seen National Assembly budget – Akpabio
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Senate President Godswill Akpabio has raised the alarm that he has never seen the budget of the National Assembly, just as he called for transparency in the area of budget. The post I’ve never seen National Assembly budget – Akpabio appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: NALDA Moves To Establish Fish Villages, Poultry Clusters Across Nigeria
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The National Agricultural Land Development Authority (NALDA) said it has taken some proactive steps towards establishing fish villages and integrated poultry clusters across the country, aimed at optimizing the potential of the subsectors of agriculture. Executive Secretary and Chief Executive Offic

item.title: ‘We’re Trapped In Nigeria’ — Carter Efe Laments After U.S. Visa Denial
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Nigerian streamer and content creator, Carter Efe, has voiced his frustration after revealing that his application for a United States visa was denied, saying he feels “trapped” in Nigeria because of the difficulty of travelling abroad. The social media personality made the remarks during a recent l

item.title: White House teleprompter operator accused of making $100k off Trump speech bets
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: A White House staffer has been accused of using inside knowledge of speeches to make nearly $100,000 on Kalshi.

item.title: Team Nigeria Wins Two Golds, One Silver At NFL Flag African Championships
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Team Nigeria has continued its dominance in African flag football, winning two gold medals and one silver at the 2026 NFL Flag American Football African Championships in Kenya. The post Team Nigeria Wins Two Golds, One Silver At NFL Flag African Championships appeared first on Trending News.
